dialogues: The Minis are collecting items! Help count what they found.
You counted the items. That's a lot! Let's see how to show this data.
Here's your data two ways. Look at both graphs. Which graph uses fewer symbols to show the same data?
Right. Scale of 10 uses fewer symbols. Each symbol shows more, so you need fewer of them.
With scale of 10, each symbol shows more than scale of 5. That's why you need fewer symbols for the same data.
Now watch this. Here's the SAME data as a bar graph.
This category has 40. The picture graph shows 4 symbols. The bar graph shows a bar up to 40. Both graphs show 50 for Birds. Can you find where?
That's it. Picture graphs use symbols. Bar graphs use bars. Both show 50.
That's it. Picture graphs use symbols. Bar graphs use bars. Both show 50.
Bar graphs are really useful with scale of 10. You read the HEIGHT instead of counting symbols.
You've seen scale of 10 with picture graphs and bar graphs. Both show the same data.
Bar graphs are powerful. You can read exact amounts from the numbers on the side.
Ready to learn how to create bar graphs with scale of 10? Let's go.
